description: 630 Correction

description: Though their bodies lie cold and dormant, the grave cannot contain the influence these seven men have had on today's world. title: A Beautiful Lie
description: How can a lie be beautiful? Set in India during Partition (when Pakistan became a separate nation), 13-year-old Bilal is dealing with the impending death of his father. He wants his father to die in peace, without knowing about the upheaval all around. An engaging story in a vivid setting at a unique time.

title: A Big Ball of String
description: Imagine all the amazing things you could do if only you had a huge ball of string! A Big Ball of String by Marion Holland is a wonderful rhyming story that shows how a boy sick in bed can still find ways to do fun things — with something as simple as string!

title: A Big Quiet House
description: With a tiny, cluttered house, giggling children, and a snoring wife, one man can't get a good night's sleep. "If only," he thinks, "I had a big quiet house!" title: A Child's Anthology of Poetry
description: Don't underestimate your children's ear for poetry. Well over two hundred great works in one rich volume—from Blake, Dickinson, and Wordsworth to Keats, Frost, and the exquisitely translated haiku of Kobayashi Issa. Let your voice shine as you read this anthology of poetry aloud together. Melodic. Hb.

title: A Child's Book of Art
description: Mostly famous, but some not-so-famous, works of art arranged in thematic patterns for young children. From medieval to modern, Western and non-Western pieces. Gorgeous and fun. "A book to grow up with." Super oversize; full-color.

title: A Christmas Carol
description: The classic story of Ebenezer Scrooge's transformation from a miserable man who hates to one who shares in Tiny Tim's joyful exclamation, "God bless us every one." A story the author, Charles Dickens, enjoyed reading every Christmas Eve. Pb. (Includes notes and additional stories.)

title: A Divine Comedy of Dante Alighieri: Inferno
description: Perhaps the most stirring masterpiece from the Middle Ages, Dante Alighieri's The Divine Comedy was an astonishing achievement that places the author among the ranks of the great epic poets of the classical world. title: A Fly Went By
description: A boy watches a fly flee from a frog, but the frog isn't chasing the fly; rather, the frog flees from the cat... and so it goes. Only when the boy finds out what causes all the flight does peace return. A Fly Went By is an early elementary reading book that features rollicking rhymes and bright illustrations.

title: A Girl Named Disaster
description: Farmer. Newbery Honor. After running away to escape an unwanted marriage, Nhamo must find her inner courage to ensure her survival of the dangerous adventure ahead.

title: A Grain of Rice
description: A humble farmer wants to marry the Emperor's daughter . . . and succeeds because of his wisdom. Delightful! China comes to life in your Sonlight reading as you enjoy this tale with your children. This story also beautifully illustrates the mathematical concept of doubling and exponential growth in a way that even child

title: A History of US - Complete Paperback Set
description: "A History of Us" by Joy Hakim is the story of the United States told through well-written, vivid, short biographies and hundreds of period illustrations, political cartoons, etchings and photographs. title: A Letter to Mrs. Roosevelt
description: When the bank forecloses on her family's home during the Great Depression, 11-year-old Margo Bandini writes to Eleanor Roosevelt for help. Fascinating plot twists and historical detail hold your attention. Heartwarming story of community cohesion and determination to do good. Powerful. Pb.

title: A Lion to Guard Us
description: Award-winning author tells a true story about three children left alone in London in 1690 who make their way to the Virginia colony. Engrossing! Pb, illus.

title: A Llama in the Family
description: Adam has had his heart set on getting a mountain bike. When his mother buys a llama to start a Vermont Trekking company instead, Adam discovers that his dreams may be too small. Heartwarming, humorous, and positive.

title: A Long Walk to Water
description: Nya walks eight hours every day to fetch water for her family. Salva walks away from his war-torn village. He is a "lost boy" refugee, destined to cover Africa on foot, searching for his family and safety. As their stories of Sudan converge, Salva decides to return to his homeland and help his people, because even in a

title: A Long Way from Chicago
description: Set in pre-Word War II America, this charming collection of short stories recounts the visits of a brother and sister to their eccentric grandmother in a small town in rural Illinois. A Long Way from Chicago by Richard Peck was a National Book Award finalist and Newbery Honor Book.

title: A Murder for Her Majesty
description: Alice, after witnessing her father's murder, flees and takes refuge with the choirboys of a large cathedral. Full of intrigue. A great story set in the Elizabethan era.

title: A Night Divided
description: Set during the time period of the rise of the Berlin Wall, the main character finds her family suddenly divided. She, her mother and brother, live on the eastern side, controlled by the Soviets. Her father and middle brother are unable to return home. A wonderfully crafted historical novel that is absorbing and deeply

title: A Parcel of Patterns
description: The Plague comes to town via a parcel very innocently bought. I love how this author writes. Pb.

title: A Passage to India
description: One of the most lyrical writers of the 20th Century, this EM Forster novel highlights the cultural differences between British colonials and their Indian counterparts. Can an Indian and a Brit be friends? Haunting. Pb.

title: A Proud Taste for Scarlet and Miniver
description: The story of Eleanor of Aquitaine waiting in heaven for her husband, King Henry II, to join her. She meets up with some old friends who reminisce about her amazing life in the Middle Ages when she was the wife of two kings and the mother of two others. Pb.

title: A Question of Yams
description: Kuri's father plants their yams in the name of Jesus Christ, though the Head Men of their village insist that they should be planted with reverence for the spirits. Will Kuri's family be punished? Is Jesus stronger than the spirits? Great story from Papua New Guinea. X Pb, illus.

title: A Rat's Tale

title: A Separate Peace
description: During World War II, two students at a New England boarding school experience the dark, tragic side of adolescence. Intense, mesmerizing, and compelling coming-of-age tale. Powerful.

title: A Severe Mercy
description: The autobiography of two pagans, deeply in love, who come to Christ. One follows wholeheartedly, the other holds back. Moving, thought-provoking, beautiful. Pb.

title: A Single Shard
description: Set in 12th-century Korea. A young orphan boy raised by a beggar under a bridge becomes an apprentice to a master potter and grows in his own talents. His journey shows faith, perseverance, and courage.

title: A Solitary Blue
description: A story about growing through the pain of betrayal and loss. Jeff's family is dysfunctional, but not as he had initially imagined. title: A Summer of Sundays
description: Eland. Middle child Sunday Fowler is too young for her sisters and too old for her brothers. How can she stand out in such a large family? Her opportunity comes when the family moves to a small town so her father can renovate the town library, and she discovers a mysterious manuscript in the basement. Charming.

title: A Tale of Two Cities
description: The French Revolution, also known as the best of times and the worst of times, are backdrops for Charles Dickens' "A Tale of Two Cities." A vivid picture of class struggles shown through the lens of two families on two continents. Compelling. Pb.

title: A Treasury of Poetry for Young People
description: This book of classic poems for students is a treasury of more than 150 works by six of America's finest poets. Beautifully illustrated collection of American poems — or poem excerpts — by Sandburg, Frost, Dickinson, Poe, Whitman, and Longfellow. Oversize Hb, full-color illus.

title: A Wrinkle in Time
description: It was a dark and stormy night when Meg Murry, brother Charles Wallace and their mom decided to have a midnight snack in the kitchen. Then a most disturbing stranger appeared. title: Abandoned on the Wild Frontier
description: Part of the Trailblazer Books series ... young Peter Cartwright Gilbert Hamilton is left alone on the frontier when his father is killed and his mother is kidnapped. The story of his life with a Methodist circuit-rider evangelist and his on-going search for his mother.

title: About Average
description: Jordan Johnston is average. Not short, not tall. Not plump, not slim. Not gifted, not flunking out. Even her shoe size is average. title: Across Five Aprils

title: Adam of the Road

title: Addition Flash Cards

title: Adoniram Judson: Bound for Burma
description: The thrilling story of America's first foreign missionary, Adoniram Judson. In the face of incredible obstacles, Adoniram and his first wife Ann were the spark that spread the fire of the Gospel into Burma. Their evident love for the Burmese people is a compelling story of unswerving dedication and sacrifice.

title: Feelings
description: A classic picture book that is a great way to explore feelings with kids. Sometimes it's hard for children to understand or explain their feelings. This book encourages dialogue about emotions.

title: Fever 1793
description: During the summer of 1793, Mattie Cook lives above the family coffee shop with her widowed mother and grandfather. title: Fidelity

title: Finding the Career that Fits You

title: Finding Truth
description: Learn the five powerful principles that penetrate to the core of any worldview from a former agnostic. See how the Christian worldview is not only true but attractive. Clearly written, well organized, and easy to understand.

title: First Animal Encyclopedia
description: Leap into the fascinating world of animals! title: First Encyclopedia of Seas and Oceans
description: Huge, salty seas and oceans make over two-thirds of Earth's surface a deep mystery. Discover this "other" world, from the coastal shores and tidal pools to the bottom of the Pacific's Mariana Trench—nearly eight miles below the waves. Get to know many unique ocean creatures including whales, sharks, plankton and pengui

title: First They Killed My Father
description: The author tells a story reflective of the experiences shared by many Cambodians who lived through the genocide under the regime of Pol Pot. title: First Thousand Words
description: Large, intricate pictures of life-scenes with thousands of words identifying everyday objects. Oversize.

title: First Thousand Words in Spanish
description: This bright and amusing book provides a wealth of vocabulary-building opportunities for beginner Spanish learners of all ages. Stephen Cartwright's delightful pictures encourage direct association of the Spanish word with the object, which will ensure effective long-term learning. First Thousand Words in Spanish offers

title: Fish in a Tree
description: Ally struggles in school, but because of frequent moves (dad is military), no one picks up on it. The author is dyslexic, and writes from a place of deep empathy for her dyslexic main character. Great friends, deep compassion, embracing your gifts-a beautiful book.

title: Flame Over Tara
description: The story of a fourteen-year-old girl in Ireland whose safe and comfortable life is turned upside down. title: Food and Nutrition for Every Kid
description: Children learn about nutrition, digestion, taste, and the nature of foods through down-to-earth explanations and fun, easy-to-do experiments. Hard to put down! Pb, illus.

title: Forces Make Things Move
description: A book all about pushing and pulling, friction, and gravity. There are forces at work whenever you throw a ball, run up the stairs, or push your big brother off the couch! Includes an experiment on friction at the end of the book.

title: Frankenstein
description: Frankenstein, written by Mary Shelley, is the original Gothic horror story that started a genre. Doctor Victor Frankenstein creates a monster that yearns for companionship. Universally spurned, the monster turns to crime. Thought-provoking. Pb.

title: Frankie Sparks and the Class Pet

title: Franklin Endicott and the Third Key
description: Frank Endicott is a worrier. He worries about lions, submarines, black holes, leprosy, and armadillos. title: Freedom Train

title: Freedom Walkers

title: Frog and Toad Together
description: Frog and Toad are back with more silly adventures. Easy to read.

title: Garbage

title: Secret Valley
description: What do you do when you've left your home in Missouri to prospect for gold in California . . . but when you arrive, there is no gold to be found? The Davis family has to face this disappointment and decide where true wealth and happiness may be found. S Pb, illus.

title: See How It's Made
description: This exciting visual tour follows the manufacture of a variety of everyday items from start to finish. Did you know glass never wears out and can be recycled forever? Did you ever wonder how toothpaste gets inside the tube? Find out as you "enter the factory" for an up close look.

title: Selecting a President
description: Selecting a President is a nuts-and-bolts look at the presidential electoral system. From the primaries to conventions to the election and Inauguaration Day, come along on the fascinating journey that begins in Iowa and New Hampshire and ends at 16 Pennsylvania Avenue.

title: Sensory Number Cards 0-10
description: These hands-on, tactile cards are an engaging way to explore numbers. title: Seven Daughters and Seven Sons
description: A true-to-life Arabic fairy tale about a poor girl who makes good through hard work, diligence . . . and brains. An all time favorite in our family. Great opportunity for discussing male-female differences and similarities. Pb.

title: Shades of Gray
description: Thought-provoking story of a boy who was orphaned by the Civil War as he struggles to forgive "the enemy" and discover what true courage is all about.

title: Shadow of a Bull
description: Son of the greatest bullfighter in Spain, Manolo Olivar wonders whether he should go along with the townspeople's plans for him to follow in his father's footsteps. At the moment of truth, he makes his decision. A moving story about a boy's growth toward manhood. Pb.

title: Shadow Spinner
description: Another one of our family's favorites, Shadow Spinner tells a plausible "story behind the story" of Scheherazade, the woman who saved her own life and the lives of all the women of Persia by telling stories to the Sultan, night after night, for 1001 nights. Exciting, fun, and thought-provoking. Pb.

title: Shakespeare's Secret
description: When sixth grade Hero (a girl named for a character in Shakespeare's Much Ado About Nothing ) moves to a new town, her elderly neighbor tells her of a missing diamond, and Hero sets out to find it, even as she learns about some of the mysteries that surround Shakespeare's writing. title: The Purpose-Driven Life: What on Earth Am I Here For?
description: Before you were born, God already planned your life. God longs for you to discover the life he uniquely created you to live—here on earth, and forever in eternity. Let The Purpose Driven Life show you how. The Purpose Driven Life is far more than just a book; it's the road map for your spiritual journey. A journey that

title: The Puzzling World of Winston Breen
description: Winston Breen loves puzzles, and he finds them everywhere-books, newspapers, even pizzas. So when Winston and his sister find some strange puzzle pieces in an antique box, it's only natural that Winston try to find out where the box came from and what the words on the puzzle pieces might mean. Soon, Winston and his fri

title: The Radical Book for Kids
description: The Radical Book for Kids by Champ Thornton is a vibrantly-illustrated introduction to the roots of your child's faith, including biblical history, theology, fun facts, and encouragements to live out their faith. This book will take children deep into the ancient roots and origins of the Christian faith. Because as chi

title: The Ramayana
description: A sweeping tale of abduction, battle, and courtship played out in a universe of deities and demons, The Ramayana is familiar to virtually every Indian. Although the Sanskrit original was composed by Valmiki around the fourth century BC, poets have produced countless versions in different languages. Here, drawing on the

title: The Ravenmaster's Secret: Escape from the Tower of London
description: Forrest works inside the Tower of London in the 1730s. He is supposed to guard the noble Maddy ... who faces execution. But will Maddy escape?

title: The Rime of the Ancient Mariner
description: A sailor kills an albatross and finds himself punished horribly as a result. Can there be forgiveness? A haunting epic ballad. Pb.

title: The Road from Home
description: The author writes a true-to-life fictionalized autobiography of his mother--a young Armenian girl in Turkey during the "other" 20th century Holocaust that was prelude to Stalin, Hitler, and others. Pb.

title: The Samurai's Tale
description: Riveting story of an orphan boy who grows up to be a Samurai in 16th century Japan. Excellent introduction to Japanese culture. Pb.

title: The Saturdays
description: The Saturdays is about four resourceful siblings who pool their allowances to create the “Independent Saturday Afternoon Adventure Club.” Set against the vibrant backdrop of 1940s New York, the novel celebrates curiosity, family bonds, financial responsibility, and personal growth.

title: The Scarlet Letter
description: A study of the tension between public and private morality, The Scarlet Letter carved new paths in American literary technique and substance. Hester Prynne, the first and arguably still greatest heroine in American literature, struggles to turn a badge that her community is determined should shame and destroy her into

title: The School Story
description: 12-year-old Natalie Nelson writes a novel that her best friend, Zoe, thinks is good enough to be published. Natalie isn't so sure. The two set out to achieve the impossible: get the book published by a major publisher. Fascinating and realistic glimpse into the world of book publishing.

title: The Screwtape Letters
description: Screwtape, a demon from the devil's own horde, has written these letters to his nephew, Wormwood, giving him tips on how to trip up the church. An eye-opening book. X Pb.

title: The Second Mrs. Gioconda
description: Multiple award winner. The riddle of the Mona Lisa is solved in a most ingenious reconstruction of the middle years of Leonardo da Vinci, the quintessential Renaissance Man. Fiction, yet based on the known facts of Leonardo's life. An absolute delight. Pb, illus. with reproductions of da Vinci's art.

title: The Secret of the Sealed Room
description: Young Patience Martin works as an indentured servant in 1721 Boston. When her mistress dies of a mysterious poisoning, Patience runs away and meets an inventive, adventurous young printer's apprentice named Ben Franklin. Can the new friends crack the case and save Patience's life? This historical fiction mystery is fil

title: The Secret Sharer & Other Stories
description: Powerful short stories about sailing and the sea. The title is about a captain who shields a stowaway, almost at the expense of his command, his boat, and his sanity. Masterful. Pb.

title: The Seventeenth Swap
description: Eric wants to buy his young crippled friend Jimmy a pair of red cowboy boots. Since he doesn't have money, he has to wheel and deal through seventeen swaps. Along the way, he learns some surprising things about himself--which make the seventeenth swap the best swap of all. S Pb.

title: The Shakespeare Stealer
description: A heartwarming story from Elizabethan England. Afraid of his master's anger but not wanting to betray his friends, Widge, an orphan, faces a crisis. Should he obey his master who commands him to steal William Shakespeare's Hamlet ? Or should he prove himself a true friend to those who have befriended him at the Globe T

title: The Sherwood Ring
description: A charming story about an orphan who goes to live with her distant uncle only to solve a long family mystery. The story pops back and forth between modern day (1950s) and the Revolutionary War with a variety of lovable and fun characters. Several memorable and witty scenes elevate this from just another magic/ fantasy

title: The Shining Company
description: A tragedy set in the context of seventh century Britain. 300 Companions seek to protect their land from invading Saxons. Told by a master storyteller. Pb.

title: The Sign of the Beaver
description: A white boy in the Maine wilderness is rescued by an Indian chief. This is the inspiring story of the boy's friendship with the chief's son. Pb.

title: The Silver Balloon
description: A captivating story about a young boy who acquires a pen pal in an unusual way. On a whim, Gregory decides to release a helium balloon with his name and address attached. The end result is a great relationship between shy Gregory and farmer Pete Mayfield.

title: The Silver Branch
description: Sutcliff. Violence and unrest are sweeping through Roman Britain. Justin and Flavius find themselves caught up in the middle of it all when they discover a plot to overthrow the Emperor. In fear for their lives, they gather together a tattered band of men and lead them into the thick of battle, to defend the honor of R

title: The Singing Tree
description: In "The Singing Tree" by Kate Seredy, life changes drastically for Kate and Jancsi when Jancsi's father is called to battle in World War I and the children are left in charge of the farm. Delightful and engaging as its predecessor, The Good Master (#HR3), but more solemn as well. Poignant. A Kate Seredy book you'll che

title: The Six Day Hero
description: Motti knows that war is coming. Israel is only nineteen years old—the same age as Motti's brave older brother, Gideon—and the tiny country is surrounded by enemies. It's only a matter of time before Egypt, Jordan and Syria attack. Motti wishes he could join the Israeli army like Gideon and be a hero. But when his best

title: The Skippack School
description: Simple but insightful story about a German boy who immigrated to America about the year 1750. One becomes dramatically aware of some of the cultural shifts that have taken place in the last 2 years! Written by a multiple award-winning author. S Pb, illus.

title: The Slopes of War
description: A young soldier from West Virginia faces the Battle of Gettysburg knowing his two cousins may be fighting him. One of the most thought-provoking books about Gettysburg. Pb.

title: The Solar System and Beyond Puzzle
description: A fun way to learn about our solar system! Learn more about the solar system while you put together this 3' long puzzle. 500 uniquely shaped pieces (not the standard "two tabs and two divots" pieces). The planets are on round pieces. As the manufacturer says, Explore the solar system with this unique puzzle while expan

title: The Squire's Tale
description: Growing up an orphan in an isolated cottage in the woods, young Terence never expected much adventure. But upon the arrival of Gawain, his life takes a surprising turn. Gawain is destined to become one of the most famous knights of the Round Table. Terence becomes Gawain's squire and leaves his secluded life for one of

title: The Story of Christianity
description: A global view of Christianity from Old Testament roots to the present day. Emphasizes events, beliefs, and practices. Lavishly illustrated. Perfect for high school students. Well-balanced presentation (from Protestant and Catholic viewpoints). X Oversize Hb.

title: The Story of Christmas
description: Pamela Dalton's exquisite, intricate scissor-cut illustrations wonderfully illuminate The Story of Christmas and accompany the text from the Gospels of Luke and Matthew. Deeply reverent, richly detailed, and teeming with life, Dalton's images follow the story of the Nativity from the appearance of the Angel, to the she

title: The Story of Inventions
description: Reveals the real-life stories behind dozens of brilliant ideas, from the invention of the wheel, to the hi-tech gadgets of today. Describes the successes - and failures - of inventions in transport, food, household gadgets, communication, clothing, medicine and more. Humorous illustrations, timelines, and internet link

title: The Story of Me (3-5 years old)
description: The Story of Me is a book that was written and illustrated for you to read with your child to help you communicate comfortably and truthfully. "Tell me my story again, Daddy," asks a little boy. And this is the story of how he came to be...beginning from God's love in making everything in the world, to the family as a

title: The Story of Science: Aristotle Leads the Way
description: A gorgeous, lavish book by a master storyteller who obviously loves her subject. Joy Hakim tells the story of scientific thought during ancient Greece and in the years following, focusing on the biographies and findings of many colorful characters. Be amazed at how far they were able to reason, without calculators or c

title: The Story of Science: Einstein Adds a New Dimension
description: Not sure you're ready for quantum and theoretical physics? You have just needed a better teacher. Hakim does an outstanding job explaining challenging concepts, and you'll understand more than you would have thought possible. Learn about the advances in scientific thought, primarily in physics. How does the world work?

title: The Story of Science: Newton at the Center
description: Picking up the story, learn about the advances in science from Newton up to the late 1800s, told mostly through fascinating short biographies of these men of science. Learn about Tycho Brahe's metal nose! About the discovery of physics and the annus mirabilis! About the feuds and partnerships, the dead-ends and the tri

title: The Story of the Amistad
description: This gripping, fast-paced book tells the dramatic story of the epic 1839 voyage of the schooner Amistad and her cargo of Africans bound for slavery in the New World. Under the leadership of Cinque, a young African farmer, the Africans revolt and seize the ship. They start for home, but instead of reaching Africa, they

title: The Story Orchestra Musical Puzzle: The Nutcracker
description: Piece together the magical adventure of Tchaikovsky’s The Nutcracker ballet with this unique musical jigsaw puzzle. Features four 10-second sound clips of an orchestra playing from Tchaikovsky’s original score—a perfect gift to introduce children to classical music. Complete the 48-piece puzzle to see how the story end

title: The Story That Cannot Be Told
description: The story of a girl finding her voice and the strength to use it during the final months of the Communist regime in Romania in 1989. Ileana has always collected stories. Some are about the past, before the leader of her country tore down her home to make room for his golden palace; back when families had enough food, a

title: The Stranger
description: Through the story of an ordinary man unwittingly drawn into a senseless murder on an Algerian beach, Camus explored what he termed "the nakedness of man faced with the absurd."

title: The Sword in the Tree
description: Lord Weldon's son and wife, displaced by a wicked uncle, appeal to King Arthur's Knights of the Round Table to see justice done. Exciting!

title: The Teacher's Funeral
description: "If your teacher has to die, August isn't a bad time of year for it." Russell Culver is fifteen in 1940, and he's raring to leave his tiny Indiana farm town for the endless sky of the Dakotas. School has been nothing but a chain holding him back from his dreams. Maybe now that his teacher has passed on, they'll shut th

title: The Tempest
description: A decade and a half after great treachery, the paths of the betrayed and the betrayer again cross. Includes a measure of mistaken identity, romance, and reconciliation. This version of the Tempest by William Shakespeare has modern English side-by-side with the original text.

title: The Terrible Two
description: Miles Murphy, the town prankster, is forced to move to a sleepy village that's famous for one thing only: cows. He quickly discovers that his new home already has a prankster. A wonderful story of joining forces and making a new friend.

title: The Thanksgiving Story
description: Caldecott Honor Book recounts the story of the Pilgrims from the time they left England on the Mayflower through the first Thanksgiving celebration. This history of Thanksgiving is told from the perspective of children, yet with careful attention to accuracy. Pb, full-color illus.

title: The Thief
description: Newbery Medal. After Gen's bragging lands him in the king's prison, his chance of escape looks slim. Then the king's scholar summons him for an impossible task—and then to reach home again, alive. A gorgeous mix of mythology, adventure, and survival, for the thoughtful reader.

title: The Thinking Toolbox
description: A follow-up to the successful The Fallacy Detective, The Thinking Toolbox is a fun and entertaining book that gives your teen the tools for clear thinking. This reasoning skills book is full of all kinds of tools that you can use for different thinking tasks; your student will learn valuable lessons like: When is it du

title: The Timeline Book
description: A blank timeline from 5,000 B.C. to the Present! Pre-printed timeline in a spiral-bound book—for use throughout your homeschooling adventure. Required Resource for Sonlight K-J and useful in 100 through 500. Don't forget to pickup the Timeline Figures that go with this history timeline book. 100 years per page 5000 BC

title: The Toothpaste Millionaire
description: A resourceful and scrupulously honest 12-year old makes a million dollars by selling toothpaste at no more than 15 cents a tube. Delightful introduction to business for young people (and older ones, too!). Pb, illus.

title: The Travel Activity Book
description: Explore over 70 countries and cultures around the world in this globetrotting activity and sticker book. Race camels through the Algerian desert, spot the difference between Bangladeshi rickshaw drivers, unscramble anagrams to name the US presidents on Mount Rushmore, and even perform the haka with the New Zealand rugb

title: The Trojan War
description: An exciting and readable version of Homer's Iliad and Odyssey, a thrilling saga of heroism and magical adventure. Pb, illus.

title: The Turning
description: Whelan. Living in Leningrad in 1991, Tanya's parents must work hard to feed their family. With the Soviet Union on the cusp of enormous change, there is plenty of politics to discuss. Tanya, a young dancer with the Kirov Ballet, considers defecting, as her friend encourages her, when the company goes to Paris.

title: The Twenty-One Balloons
description: An inventive scientist lands on Krakatoa only days before the volcano blows. The Twenty One Balloons by William Pene du Bois a Utopian tale with great illustrations.

title: The U.S. Supreme Court
description: How do cases make their way to the Supreme Court? How do the justices come to their final decision? In this brief introductory text, a Pulitzer Prize winning journalist explains how the Supreme Court really works.

title: The Universe Next Door
description: This accessible title introduces your children to nine prominent worldviews, such as deism, existentialism, naturalism and nihilism.

title: The Unteachables
description: Burned out teacher Zachary Kermit has one more year before early retirement. With a class of outcasts and misfits and delinquents, this seems like a recipe for disaster, but ends up with a lot of growth and redemption. Humorous and thoughtful.

title: The Usborne Book of World History
description: Required Resource for History / Bible / Literature C. (Included in B and B+C. If you purchase History / Bible / Literature C and have not used B or B+C, you will need to purchase this book separately.) The history of the world to 1914. Includes cultural developments, discoveries, and world religions as well as politica

title: The Usborne Time Traveler
description: Required Resource for History / Bible / Literature C. (Included in B and B+C. If you purchase History / Bible / Literature C and have not used B or B+C, you will need to purchase this book separately.) Visit Ancient Egypt, Rome, medieval Europe and Viking days. Colorful, detail-packed illustrations teach about family l

title: The Usborne World Of Animals
description: From the Himalayan Mountains to the Namib desert, you and your children take a grand tour of the animal kingdom. You'll learn about the habitats and habits of creatures from all over the world. Stunning photography and engaging text. Oversized.

title: The Van Gogh Deception
description: A forgery mystery, with a boy with amnesia and effective and thorough creeps who are trying to rip off a museum for $189 million.

title: The Vanderbeekers of 141st Street
description: Get 30% off this book with your purchase of a Sonlight Advent Unit Study . Offer available while supplies last through October 13, 2023.* --> It’s five days before Christmas and the Vanderbeeker children — Isa, Jessie, Oliver, Hyacinth, and Laney — should be dreaming about sugar plums and presents. But when Mr. Beiderm

title: The Very First Americans
description: Learn all about the very first Americans, long before Columbus landed. Watercolor paintings depict the clothing, dwellings, art, tools, and other Native American artifacts.

title: The Victory of Reason
description: Sociologist Rodney Stark makes the case that Christianity positively influenced the development of a variety of globally significant movements in relation to democracy, science, reason and more. Far from being a negative influence or a backwards religion, Stark convincingly argues that Christians and Christianity are r

title: The View from Saturday
description: Four friends from a class of social outcasts attempt to win an Academic Bowl with the help of their encouraging teacher. Sarita says, "As a two-time Newbery Medal winner, you know she has a good writing style. But this book has a wonderful story-line as well. It has the best of all worlds mixed into one."

title: The Viking Adventure
description: Sigurd, a Viking boy, cannot see the value of learning to read and write. All he can think of is adventure. But then he has an adventure that he cannot help but tell. And to do that, he decides, he must learn to write. A fine story for young readers. S. Pb, illus.

title: The War That Saved My Life
description: Bradley. Newbery Honor. A daughter and son, raised by an abusive mother, go to live with a grieving woman in the country when the children of London are evacuated during WWII. There, all three try to recover from their inner wounds. Bits of the Battle of Dunkirk, the Battle of Britain, what it was like to catch spies,

title: The Watsons Go to Birmingham—1963
description: When the Watson family—ten-year-old Kenny, Momma, Dad, little sister Joetta, and brother Byron—sets out on a trip south to visit Grandma in Birmingham, Alabama, they don't realize that they're heading toward one of the darkest moments in America's history. The Watsons' journey reminds us that even in the hardest times,

title: The Wave
description: When a history teacher tests the power of peer pressure, his experiment gains frightening momentum beyond his control. Two students, Laurie and David, realize they must stop a dangerous movement before it's too late. Let this sobering drama, based on actual events, spark thoughtful discussion with your family.

title: The Wednesday Wars
description: Holling is a 7th-grade boy forced to spend Wednesday afternoons dissecting Shakespeare with his teacher who, he is certain, "hates his guts." You'll grow with him through the uproars of adolescence and 1960s culture, as Holling realizes that he can choose the life he wants. Glorious.

title: The Westing Game
description: The only mystery story ever to win the Newbery Medal! Sixteen players are possible heirs to Sam Westing's fortune . . . and all of them are suspects in the case of his curious demise. The one who discovers the villain's identity wins $200 million; will the losers lose all? A pleasurable read.

title: The Westminster Shorter Catechism
description: This illustrated manual offers clear exposition of each of the 107 questions and answers in this historic evangelical teaching tool. X Pb.

title: The Whipping Boy
description: The Whipping Boy by Sid Fleischman focuses upon a prince who has to learn the hard way what makes for true character. He'd never known, since his "whipping boy" had taken all the punishment for him whenever he failed to do what was right. Delightfully thought-provoking. Pb, illus.

title: The White House Is Burning
description: This "biography of a single day" captures the burning of the White House by the British during the War of 1812 from the viewpoint of the people who were there, including First Lady Dolley Madison, a British officer, and a nine-year-old slave. In the early hours before dawn, a cry went out that the British were advancin

title: The Winged Watchman
description: An historically accurate portrait of family life in the polders and among the windmills in Holland during the last years of Nazi occupation during WWII . . . and the months after, as families began to come back together. How do you avoid bitterness amidst overwhelming evil? Wonderful view of family life in another cult

title: The Wise Woman and Other Stories
description: Four wonderful tales written by the man whom C.S. Lewis considered one of the world's best Christian authors. X Pb.

title: The Witch of Blackbird Pond
description: A girl raised in luxury in the Caribbean struggles to adjust to Puritan New England. She befriends a lonely Quaker widow whom some call "the witch of Blackbird Pond." There is no witch or witchcraft in this honest and inspiring story of Puritan life. Pb.

title: The Wizards of Once: Twice Magic
description: This was once the story of a young boy Wizard and a young girl Warrior who had been taught since birth to hate each other like poison. But now, the boy Wizard and girl Warrior have been brought together in the Badwoods and they have witnessed the shocking consequences of the Stone That Takes Away Magic. They will need

title: The Wolves of Willoughby Chase
description: Wolves without and a wicked governess within lead two English girls, Bonnie and Sylvia, to many spine-tingling adventures. Suspenseful. Fun!

title: The Wondrous World of Violet Barnaby
description: Eighteen months after a girl's mother died of cancer, her dad quickly marries the meanest teacher in school, and Violet acquires two step-siblings. She finds a hidden letter from her mom, with a list of things to do to celebrate Christmas. Good look at family, and how to deal with emotions and hurt feelings and pain an

title: The World According to Humphrey
description: Experience the world through the eyes of a young hamster who learns about families and cultures and friendship and kindness. Humphrey spends the week in a classroom, and each weekend travels to the home of a different student. A great story about encouraging others.

title: The World Wars
description: Take a deep breath and explore both conflicts in one sobering, compelling book—from the soldiers' terrifying experiences in trench warfare to mighty and sinister innovations that shaped the course of history. Dramatic photographs and detailed maps help make this an accessible, thought-provoking view of the most destruc

title: The Wounds of God
description: The second book in the series following the lives of the brothers of St. Alcuin, a medieval monastery. This series explores the love of God for man, as well as the love between brothers in Christ. See how monastic life reflects the human struggle of every person who desires to love God and walk in grace.

title: The Wright Brothers
description: A delightful account of the exploits, the family life, and the character of the two bike builders who are generally credited with having been the first to engage in powered flight. Pb.

title: The Yanks Are Coming
description: A gripping account of how the United States joined WWI and helped turn the tide of the entire war. This fascinating narrative brings the war to life and reveals how the US mobilized industry, trained "doughboy" soldiers, and promoted the war at home. Portrays the deep human cost of the war as well as the heroic actions

title: The Year at Maple Hill Farm
description: Watch the seasons change on an upstate New York farm. Observe how they affect the natural world. Oversize; full-color.

title: The Year Of the Baby
description: Anna learned how to be a good friend last year. This year, she wants to learn how to be a good sister to a baby girl her family adopted from China. When the doctor warns that the baby isn't thriving, Anna and her best friends, Laura and Camille, create a science project to help save the little one. Will Anna's devotion

title: The Year of the Dog
description: It's the Chinese Year of the Dog, and as Pacy celebrates with her family, she finds out that this is the year she is supposed to "find herself." Universal themes of friendship, family, and finding one's passion in life make this novel appealing to readers of all backgrounds. This funny and profound book is a wonderful

title: Their Eyes Were Watching God
description: A powerful tale of one woman's life. Set in black communities in the Deep South in the early 1900s, and told with a stunning, deeply moving, almost poetic prose. Hurston breathes life into her characters and lets them speak for themselves. A true literary classic.

title: Then and Now
description: Simple text provides discussion starters to help children understand historical time, a concept difficult to grasp.

title: Theodore Boone: Kid Lawyer
description: In the small city of Strattenburg, there are many lawyers, and though he's only 13 years old, Theo Boone thinks he's one of them. Theo knows every judge, policeman, court clerk and a lot about the law. He dreams of a life in the courtroom. But he finds himself in court much sooner than expected. Because he knows so muc

title: Theras and His Town
description: A young boy in fifth-century Greece was raised in Athens and then separated from his family and forced to live in Sparta. This book includes danger, adventure, courage and triumph. Pb.

title: There's a Sheep In My Bathtub
description: What can God do when you simply trust and obey Him? Watch an unreached people explode with the Gospel—not in the first century, but in ours! Like this American family in remote, mysterious Outer Mongolia, you will believe God can still do the impossible.

title: They Loved to Laugh
description: In the 1830s, a young orphan with grave blue eyes heads to a new life in North Carolina. As she learns to adapt to her new Quaker surroundings, her new family and friends wonder if this solemn girl will ever learn to laugh. "They Loved to Laugh" is one of Sarita's favorite books from her childhood. Discover our selecti

title: Thimble Summer
description: Newbery Medal winner about a mid-American farm family in the early 20th century. Charming. Pb.

title: Things Fall Apart
description: Things Fall Apart tells two intertwining stories, both centering on Okonkwo, a "strong man" of an Ibo village in Nigeria. The first, a powerful fable of the immemorial conflict between the individual and society, traces Okonkwo's fall from grace with the tribal world. The second, as modern as the first is ancient, conc

title: Think Analogies® B1
description: Looking for an easy-to-understand method to teach analogies with plenty of practice? This 64-page book teaches students how to analyze and classify analogies by relationship, category, and structure. Students who learn strategies to make and complete analogies benefit in many ways. They sharpen their reasoning skills a

title: Thinkwell Economics Internet Course
description: An incredible Internet-based resource for teaching your high schoolers about Modern Economics—both macro and micro. Thinkwell combines dynamic online multimedia video lectures and interactive exercises for a powerful learning experience. Please Note: Thinkwell subscriptions cannot be returned if the packaging has been

title: Thinkwell Public Speaking
description: Do you have a fear of public speaking? Do you need help preparing for class or work presentations? Learn the art of public speaking with Thinkwell's Public Speaking study aid, so that no matter when you need to speak in front of a crowd, whether it's a toast for a friend's wedding, an oral report for class, or a high-s

title: Third Grade Detectives #1 and #2
description: This book has two mysteries: The Clue of the Left-Hand Envelope and The Puzzle of the Pretty Pink Handkerchief. In the first mystery, someone sent Amber a secret-admirer letter, and Noelle and Todd want to find out who did it. In the second, Todd uses clues to find out which one of his schoolmates has been in his tree

title: Third Grade Detectives #10
description: When a valuable fifteenth-century statue of Joan of Arc is destroyed at the local art museum, it looks like a case for the Third-Grade Detectives! Who would do it, and why? Pb, illus.

title: Third Grade Detectives #4
description: A thief is in town and it's up to the Third-Grade Detectives to solve the case before everyone in class loses their stuff! Pb. Illus.

title: This Book Thinks You're a Math Genius
description: This fill-in book helps children to think like mathematicians by introducing key mathematical concepts in a highly visual–and entertaining–way. Through fun activities and illustrations, This Book Thinks You're a Math Genius encourages young readers to engage with new ideas by experimenting and investigating for themsel

title: This Book Thinks You're a Scientist
description: A creative fill-in educational activity book that encourages children to think like a scientist by noticing details, questioning everything and–crucially–dreaming up new ideas. Developed by the Science Museum, London, as a complement to their new interactive gallery for children, this book explores seven key scientific

title: This Book Thinks You're an Artist
description: Brimming with humorous and creative tasks to do on and off the page, this fill-in book will encourage children to think like an artist. This Book Thinks You're an Artist combines art history with practical activities on the page. There are sculptures to build, portraits to draw, selfies to color and manifestos to write

title: Three Little Piggies
description: A perfect brain game for young children (starting as young as 3), this features three big puzzle pieces, a storybook with images, and a booklet with 48 challenges. Can you help these three smart pigs build their houses? Can you set them up so that the pigs can play outside? If you spot the wolf, can you help the pigs s

title: Three Men in a Boat
description: Three friends decide to spend a week relaxing in a boat on the Thames. Forget the relaxation! The author's dry humor and insightful commentary — compared favorably to Mark Twain's! — will keep you in stitches. One of those books you don't want to put down. Pb.

title: Three Young Pilgrims
description: Based on the true story of Remember, Mary and Bartholomew Allerton. Experience long days at sea aboard the Mayflower followed by the first winter in the New World. Richly detailed paintings bring the pilgrim's first months to life, culminating with the original Thanksgiving feast.

title: Tiger Boy
description: "One of the new tiger cubs has escaped from the reserve!" When a tiger cub escapes from a nature reserve near Neel's island village, the rangers and villagers hurry to find her before the cub's anxious mother follows suit and endangers them all. Mr. Gupta, a rich newcomer to the island, is also searching—he wants to se

title: Tikta'Liktak
description: A compelling and vivid story of survival in the Arctic. Wonderful portrayal of Inuit (Eskimo) culture. Pb, illus.

title: Till We Have Faces
description: A masterful retelling of the Cupid and Psyche myth presented with a Christian twist as only C.S. Lewis could do it. X Pb.

title: Timeline Figures A
description: All the Timeline Figures needed for Sonlight A on self-adhesive heavy card stock. Just cut them out, color and stick them in your Timeline Book .

description: Over 40 Timeline Figures for Sonlight B on self-adhesive heavy card stock. Just cut them out, color and stick them in your Timeline Book . description: 90 Timeline Figures for Sonlight B+C on self-adhesive heavy card stock. Just cut them out, color and stick them in your Timeline Book .
